title: Simira rubra
intro: Simira rubra, also known as Caesalpinia ferrea or Brazil ironwood, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, characterized by its unique reddish-brown wood and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anemia, due to its high iron content, which makes it an effective remedy for iron deficiency. The bioactive constituents of Simira rubra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Simira rubra include teas, infusions, and supplements, which are often used to support healthy iron levels and improve overall well-being.
taxonomy: Simira rubra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Simira, which is a group of plants commonly found in tropical regions. The species is native to tropical Africa. Further classification includes its kingdom as Plantae, its order as Fabales, and its family as Fabaceae. Its classification is also known as Caesalpinioid clade.
